what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment (att-5ed0ecd4) in the design-source channel, linking it to the image URI image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-37 and logging when it was added.
observations: File body is empty after the frontmatter.
The id field matches the filename stem att-5ed0ecd4.
standouts: It is a pure metadata stub with no content beyond frontmatter.
The target URI names a specific pitch identifier p-37 under recent-pitches.
concerns: No title, caption, or descriptive text for the image is present.
No author or source context is recorded.
The target URI cannot be resolved from this file alone.
conventions: References a channel memory via a channel field and an image via an image:// URI scheme.
ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit UTC offset.
notes: This is a minimal attachment ledger entry that points to an image asset in the design-source channel.
questions: Is p-37 a pitch document, a slide deck, or a single image?
Who added this attachment and why?
Are there other attachments related to p-37?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as an attachment ledger entry; its content matches that purpose, though it carries only identifiers and no descriptive payload.
intention_signals: It seems intended to register an image asset against a design-source channel memory so the image can be retrieved in the context of recent pitches.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped attachment record that maps a channel memory entry to an image asset URI, enabling retrieval and association of visual pitch material with the design-source channel.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata file att-5ed0ecd4 in the design-source channel that references image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-37 and records an addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register an image attachment for a recent pitch asset in the design-source channel memory.
